translated: [CORNER: Suggestion Box] It's time for the Yuigaoka Girls' High School Suggestion Box, where we read your letters.
This is the Love Live! Superstar!! Yuigaoka Lunchtime Broadcasting Station, Lieraji!
Now, everyone say it with us! One, two... Suggestion Box!
Let's get right into the emails.
First, we have one from Liella name: Popcorn Who Wanted to Pop.
Thank you very much.
Thank you!
"I attended the 7th Live performance in Kanagawa."
"It was truly a blast."
"We got to see the solo songs for the first time, and Kanon-chan's 'Over Over' was especially hype!"
"Kanon-chan has always struggled with her feelings toward singing, so seeing her perform so happily..."
"It made me emotional realizing she reaffirmed her love for it through Season 3 of the anime."
"Her energy during the call-and-response really took my excitement to the max!"
Thank you so much.
Thank you!
Next, we have one from Liella name: Mattsukutsui.
Thank you very much.
Thank you!
"Kanon-chan's 'Over Over' was so exciting!"
"I only realized this after seeing it live, but isn't that song incredibly difficult to sing?"
"Seeing Kanon-chan running around the stage with so much energy made me so happy,"
"but I also felt like she was singing at full power the entire time."
"I really felt like that song requires an immense amount of energy to pull off."
That's what they wrote.
Thank you so much.
Thank you!
The solo songs...
Man, they were great.
Seriously.
I'm so happy.
I'm glad.
It's like...
I'm just so grateful.
Regarding 'Over Over'...
Kanon's past solo songs have had a lot of different vibes,
but I think this one was the most hype—or rather, it was like 150% pure positivity.
I was so happy to perform it.
It was amazing.
And you're right, it's actually a very difficult song to sing.
It's like being at full throttle the whole time. But since Kanon...
loves singing so much, she's always used a handheld mic,
and this time was no different. She gripped that mic tight,
feeling the blue sky pushing her forward as she sang.
So I, Sayuri, wanted to sing while feeling like I was that blue sky,
pushing Kanon forward from behind.
But to do that, I had to become the blue sky myself.
I felt like I had to go even beyond Kanon's level.
I don't know if "above" or "below" is the right way to put it,
but I felt like I needed to have more confidence than ever to support her.
That feeling made me so happy.
Also, I ran around the stage a lot to go see everyone in the audience.
Even though it's Kanon's song,
the lyrics use words like "we."
It's not just about her; it's about "how far can we go?"
It felt like she was singing to you, and with you.
So I made a conscious effort to look at the audience rather than the cameras.
I could tell.
That was so much fun, it just naturally made me smile.
I was genuinely enjoying myself.
That's lovely.
Like Sayuri said,
the feeling that it wasn't just about her really came across.
That's why everyone was cheering so loudly along with her.
Even though it's Kanon-chan's solo,
it felt like a song for everyone, created by everyone. That sense of unity was incredible.
I thought, "Wow, that looks like so much fun." It was beautiful.
It really was.
The lighting staff were so detailed too; they used marigold colors,
and they also used blue to represent the sky.
I was so happy they put so much thought into it.
Yeah.
I'm so grateful. It was a blast.
It was seriously so good.
I was actually really moved.
Like...
The tempo of the song is basically a full-on sprint the whole time.
It's not the kind of song that usually makes you feel quiet and emotional,
but even though it's so bright, it was somehow deeply moving.
Watching from backstage...
Even though my solo song is totally different from Kanon-chan's...
